entry.title: Re: Future dated onboardees cannot be searched via Inacti...
entry.content:
Hi  ,

Thank you for your follow-up. I understand that you're unable to locate the specific permission under Employee Data in Role-Based Permissions (RBP) to enable searching for inactive employees. Based on the information provided and the available resources, here's the exact permission you need to enable:

Required Permission:

  • Permission Name: Include Inactive Employees in the search
  • Location: Admin Center > Manage Permission Roles > [Select Role] > Permissions > Manage Hires or Manage Recruits

Enabling this permission allows users with the assigned role to search for inactive employees, including future-dated hires, within the Employee Profile.

Additional Considerations:

  • Global Search Header Visibility: If the "Hide External Users from Search Results" option is enabled in Company System and Logo Settings, future-dated hires will not appear in the global search header. However, they can still be searched within the Employee Profile if the above permission is granted.
  • Role Assignment: Ensure that the permission is enabled for the appropriate roles (e.g., HR, Admin) and that these roles are assigned to the relevant users.
  • Testing: After configuring the permissions, test the functionality by searching for a future-dated hire within the Employee Profile to confirm that the permissions are correctly applied.

If you continue to experience issues or need further assistance, please provide additional details about your current configuration, and I'll be glad to help you troubleshoot further.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: No data is available for the chart to render. SAC
entry.content:
Its a Live connection, check for variable change in the back end. If need to maintain appropriate values to those variables maintain them. 

Check for Filters applied on this widget and adjust if necessary to display your data.

Correct if any overlapping  filters that restricts the data.

In analyzer see the data with the same measures/dimensions in your widget and Create a test widget with same set of designer controls and see if the data coming there and you can easily understand the reason why data is not showing here.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Regional and Local Premium Engagements Events
entry.content:
Regional and Local Premium Engagements Events

We are delighted to inform you about the upcoming regional and local Premium Engagements event series that provides invited customers with a unique opportunity to gain first-hand insights from SAP experts on key topics such as SAP Business Suite, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Business AI, RISE with SAP, clean core, and enterprise architecture. Each one- or two-day event offers a deep dive into key topics covered at the SAP MaxAttention Summit and demonstrates the central role SAP MaxAttention and premium engagements plays in the success of SAP’s top customers.

Attendees will be given extensive opportunities to network and will have the chance to share their experiences with like-minded peers in the Premium Engagements community. The events feature keynotes and breakout sessions with SAP executives, experts, and customers, allowing attendees to engage with each other and gain valuable insights from industry professionals.

Please find an overview of all currently announced regional and local Premium Engagements Forums 2025 below. Updates will continuously be added to the respective pages.

  • APAC (GC): July 9 in Beijing
  • APAC (APJ): August 26-28 in Kuala Lumpur
  • APAC (ANZ): September 4-5 in Sydney
  • AMERICAS (NA): October 28-29 in NSQ
  • AMERICAS (Mexico): November 4 in Mexico City
  • AMERICAS (Brazil): November 6-7 in Sao Leopoldo

Save-the-date emails and event invites will be sent to all Premium Engagements customers. We encourage you to register and reserve a spot in your calendar as soon as you receive your invitation.

For our Market Units in EMEA and MEE there will also be a few smaller events with the first events kicking off in June.

EMEA

  • Madrid, June 12
  • Paris, June 19
  • Milan, June 26
  • Johannesburg, July 8
  • Cape Town, July 10
  • Dubai, July 16
  • Hertogenbosch, September 11
  • Istanbul, September 25

MEE

  • Heidelberg, June 24
  • Zurich, July 1
  • Vienna, September 24

We look forward to welcoming you to the regional and local events and driving transformation success together. If you have any questions, feel free to post them in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: No data is available for the chart to render. SAC
entry.content: Hi 07makita, It looks like the chart isn’t rendering because no data is available at the moment. Since you’re using a live BW connection, sometimes data might not load due to connectivity issues or changes in the BW queries. Here are a few steps you could try: Check the BW query to make sure the key figures are still available and haven’t been modified or deleted. Verify the data source connection in SAC to ensure it’s still active and authorized. Refresh the data manually or try to reload the model in SAC. Confirm that any filters or variables applied in your SAC story aren’t excluding all data by mistake. If all else fails, consider reaching out to your BW admin to check if there are any backend issues or recent changes. Hope this helps! Phonetics tip for pronunciation: SAC — say as “Ess-Ay-See” BW — say as “Bee-Double-You” Good luck fixing it!

entry.title: Re: Message Validation Policy of API Management is not va...
entry.content:
try add <SOAPMessage version= "1.1/1.2" /> to your policy. 

<MessageValidation async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
<Element namespace="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions">ZSD_ESALES_TRUCKS_LIST</Element>
<SOAPMessage version= "1.1/1.2" />
<Source>request</Source>
<ResourceURL>wsdl://ZSD_ESALES_TRUCKS_LIST.wsdl</ResourceURL>
</MessageValidation>

entry.title: Re: EWM PPF Change Output Device
entry.content:
Hi,

If it is a label or form from HU then you can maintain a printer in the condition record in transaction /scwm/gcmc you can also maintain a printer to a user in SU3 that also should work.

If the form is related to delivery or TU you can maintain the printer in /SCWM/SR_PRINT and also in SU3.

entry.title: Connect, Automate, and Thrive with 4 SAP BTP Use Cases You Cannot Miss at Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Mark your calendars—SAP Sapphire 2025 is just around the corner (or you should already have the calendars marked and starting to pack). Sapphire 2025 will showcase SAP's strategic vision with SAP Business Suite, new innovations like Joule Agents, and industry-leading solutions in action. Whether you're seeking strategic insights or hands-on technology demonstrations, Sapphire 2025 promises to deliver value that extends well beyond the event itself.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform: The Backbone of Intelligent Enterprises

In this blog, I'll explore the breakthrough use cases for 2025 that demonstrate SAP's commitment to creating fully integrated business processes. We'll see how the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the essential foundation connecting disparate systems, processes, and data flows to deliver transformative business outcomes. Each use case is supported by comprehensive Discovery Center Missions and Business Accelerators designed to fast-track your implementation and maximize value creation.
For attendees joining us in person, our expert-led sessions will provide deep dives on how to get started with SAP BTP. Our specialists will also be available at the SAP BTP for Business Application booth to address your specific business challenges and help architect your connected enterprise vision. You don’t want to miss the following sessions in Orlando to learn more about the SAP BTP use cases and SAP Discovery Center, so add them to your agenda right now.


BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center

 

New Business Technology Platform Use Cases: Unlocking End-to-End Connectivity 

In today's complex business environment, siloed operations and error-prone, fragmented processes create significant barriers to efficiency and growth. The true power of Intelligent Enterprise lies in seamless connectivity across your entire business landscape and utilizing the unified landscape to automate processes and apply insights analysis. Let's explore four new SAP BTP use cases for 2025 that will help your organization get started:

 

Use Case #1: Connect all applications across the business with SAP Integration Suite

• The Challenge: Organizations today operate with complex landscapes spanning cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. Critical business processes often span multiple applications, both from SAP and from other vendors. creating fragmentation that leads to inefficiencies and data inconsistencies. These fragmented processes add manual work, increase maintenance costs, and limit real-time access to valuable data.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP Integration Suite provides a comprehensive integration platform that seamlessly connects your entire enterprise ecosystem—from SAP Business Suite applications to third-party solutions, legacy systems, and emerging technologies. The platform's pre-built integration content significantly accelerates implementation time, while its API management capabilities allow you to securely expose business processes to partners and developers.

By creating unified, end-to-end process flows, organizations can eliminate data silos and ensure consistent information across systems. Real-time data monitoring provides visibility into cross-system records, enabling proactive management of business operations. The outcome is a connected business environment where information flows across your enterprise landscape, driving efficiency. CONA Services is a great example of an organization that was able to minimize data errors and increase master data accuracy with SAP Integration Suite.

 

Use Case #2: Drive Your Business with Integrated Enterprise Performance Management

• The Challenge: Financial planning and performance management processes frequently operate in isolation from operational data sources. Finance teams spend excessive time collecting and reconciling data across systems rather than focusing on strategic analysis. This disconnect between financial planning and operational execution creates misalignment between corporate objectives and day-to-day business activities.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP's integrated Enterprise Performance Management solution on BTP creates a seamless connection between financial planning and operational execution. By leveraging SAP BTP capabilities, organizations can assess new opportunities, improve profitability, and respond with greater agility in the face of unexpected change and disruption. It brings together data from Cloud ERP and other business applications to create a unified planning and performance management analysis with SAP Analytics Cloud.

This connected approach enables continuous planning cycles that adapt to changing business conditions. Operational data flows automatically into financial planning models, eliminating manual data collection and reconciliation efforts. Business leaders gain real-time visibility into how operational decisions impact financial outcomes, while predictive capabilities highlight future opportunities and risks. The result is truly connected planning where strategic objectives drive operational execution, and performance insights inform strategic adjustments in a continuous feedback loop. Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions shares how they are aligning their operational planning with financial performance across multiple regions and business functions.

 

Use Case #3: Enhance Employee Onboarding Experience Through Streamlined Processes with AI

• The Challenge: The employee onboarding journey typically requires multiple people across multiple departments and systems—from HR and IT to facilities management and department-specific applications before the employee starts. From using document collection and antiquated paper-based verification to manual data errors, this fragmentation creates a poor experience for the new hire before they’ve even started and reduces productivity during the critical first months, while also increasing administrative burden across the organization. HR teams struggle to orchestrate complex cross-departmental workflows, while managers lack visibility into the onboarding progress.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP BTP enables a revolutionary approach to employee onboarding by connecting previously isolated systems and processes into a unified experience. Through SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, organizations can create a single-entry point for all onboarding activities, while BTP's workflow management capabilities orchestrate tasks across systems and departments.

The solution connects SAP SuccessFactors with identity management, equipment provisioning, and department-specific processes and applications to create a seamless onboarding journey. New employees experience a coherent process regardless of the underlying systems, while HR teams and managers gain real-time visibility into onboarding progress and can proactively address potential delays. By connecting the entire onboarding ecosystem, organizations significantly reduce time-to-productivity for new hires while decreasing administrative overhead, ultimately transforming onboarding from a series of disconnected tasks to a strategic advantage in talent management. Gerdau S.A. demonstrates how this innovation not only improved the efficiency of the hiring process by leveraging the power of AI to reduce or eliminate manual data entry and review, but also improved the onboarding experience for both candidates and HR.

 

Use Case #4: Unlock Enterprise Automation with the Power of SAP BTP and SAP Signavio

• The Challenge: Business process automation initiatives often focus on isolated processes without considering end-to-end business outcomes. Organizations struggle to identify which processes should be prioritized for automation and how to ensure automated processes remain aligned with business objectives. After implementation, it's difficult to measure the actual impact of automation on business performance.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: The combination of SAP Signavio and SAP BTP creates a powerful solution for intelligent, end-to-end process automation. Signavio's process mining and business process management capabilities enable organizations to visualize current processes, identify bottlenecks, and simulate the impact of potential changes. This intelligence then guides automation implementation through BTP's comprehensive suite of automation tools, including SAP Build Process Automation and integration capabilities.

This integrated approach enables a continuous process improvement cycle: processes are analyzed, optimized, automated, and monitored in a connected loop. Organizations can prioritize automation initiatives based on business impact, implement them seamlessly across integrated systems, and measure the resulting outcomes accurately. By connecting process insights directly to automation implementation, businesses can achieve transformative results such as reduced cycle times, improved compliance, enhanced customer experiences, and significant cost savings while maintaining alignment with strategic objectives. Blue Diamond Growers’ prioritization of business values and elimination of process gaps with the insights from SAP Signavio allowed them to identify the most impactful automation opportunities.

 

“Must-Attend/Watch” SAP BTP Sessions

  • BTP2123 | Unleash the power of your applications, data, and AI with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Michael Ameling
  • BTP2293 | Real stories, real impact: How customers are driving success with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Bharat Sandhu
  • BTP2122: Explore the Roadmap for SAP Business Technology Platform
    Orlando | Virtual Speaker: Steffen Pietsch
  • BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
  • BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center
  • BTP2075 | Navigate the path to autonomous finance with SAP Business Suite and SAP BTP
  • BTP2072 | Discover how SAP customers are realizing business value with SAP BTP
  • BTP2074 | Maximize your move to SAP cloud ERP and SAP Business Suite with SAP BTP
  • BTP2468 | SAP BTP: The center of a clean core strategy for long-term success

 

To Wrap Up


As we look toward Sapphire 2025, it's clear that end-to-end connectivity is no longer just a technical consideration—it's a strategic imperative for business success. Whether you're focusing on enterprise integration, connected planning, streamlined employee experiences, or intelligent process automation, SAP Business Technology Platform stands as the essential foundation of SAP Business Suite. It connects systems, processes, and experiences across the enterprise landscape, enabling truly integrated business operations.
Visit the SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for comprehensive resources on these use cases and join us at Sapphire 2025 to experience firsthand how SAP BTP is redefining what's possible through end-to-end connectivity. Together, we'll explore how your organization can become truly connected, intelligent, and ready for whatever the future holds.

entry.title: Re: Which function call returns 0? in sap abap
entry.content:
Dear Sushmithanaveenanthea,


ABAP has several functions that return 0.

Example 1:

Returns the length of a string as an integer (type i). If you call it with an empty string or an initial string variable, you will receive exactly 0.
DATA(lv_zero) = strlen( '' ). " lv_zero = 0

Example 2:

Returns the number of rows in an internal table as an integer. If the table is empty, the result is also 0.
DATA(lv_zero) = lines( itab_empty ). " lv_zero = 0

Example 3: Index Function for Table Expressions

If you access the table via an index that doesn't exist, the expression function returns 0:

DATA: lt_outer TYPE TABLE OF ty_inner,
lt_inner TYPE TABLE OF ty_line.
lt_outer = VALUE #( ( lt_inner ) ). "Outer table with an entry that itself contains an empty lt_inner table
WRITE / lt_outer[ 2 ]. "returns 0 because there is no second row in lt_outer

There are many more examples where 0 is returned.

I hope these examples will help you.

Best regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Re: Recuriting candidates' group mail
entry.content:
You can send emails to more than 10 candidates at once. First, increase the number of items per page using the dropdown menu at the bottom. Then, select all the candidates and click the Send Email button. You should be able to see all the selected candidates on the next page.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Central Job Scheduling - Successor for SAP Solution M...
entry.content:
Hi, jan_kotrc,

I believe you found the question/answer you pose from FAQ at Job & Automation Monitoring. 

If you check a lower question on the same page:
What about Job Management in SAP Cloud ALM (similar to SAP Solution Manager)?
It states:

Question: We use SAP Solution Manager for Job Scheduling Request Management and document the scheduled jobs (using the versioning option offered). We have integration with an external ticketing system and external scheduling. Will we be able to use SAP Cloud ALM to replace SAP Solution Manager?

Answer: It is not on the roadmap of SAP Cloud ALM standard to replace all of SAP Solution Manager's Job Management capabilities, but the following Job Management is possible:

  • In SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation one can (see here(opens in new tab)) 
    • request the periodic execution of jobs and automations
    • document the approved periodic executions, link them to implemented business processes and reference them to Cloud ALM job monitoring.
  • In SAP Cloud ALM for Operations (Job & Automation Monitoring) one can (see here(opens in new tab))
    • get alerted, if there is a job scheduled by a not allowed user
    • get alerted, if any job that is scheduled by allowed users fails or exceeds its runtime limit
    • (soon) see in a dashboard the current status of all documented jobs and have access to the related job documentation 

There is further info there you can review.

In case the above does not meet your needs, you would need to look at a 3rd party Solution.

Just because you are considering from on-premise point-of-view, I add the following: for Cloud, there is the SAP Job Scheduling Service, but that is not for on premise systems.

(As always, you are welcome to share your ideas for possible functionality in the Influence Campaign:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

As far as using SAP Solution Manager past 2027, it appears you can, however, support will be limited. We assume some customers are going to stay with Solman until 2030. Beyond that you will need to find another solution as there will not be parody between Solution Manager and Cloud ALM features.

I hope this is helpful information for you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 15th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast : https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/15/sap-developer-news-may-15th-2025/ 

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) April release 

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/apr25  

May Developer Challenge Mid-month Promotion 

  • Blogpost: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-community-escape-house-for-may-abap-cloud/bc-p/14099138#M49846  

Sapphire Promo / Mini CodeJam Roadshow in the US in June 

  • Sapphire: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire.html 
  • New York AI CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-new-york-new-york-usa/ec-p/14099369#M838 
  • Palo Alto AI CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-palo-alto-ca-usa/ec-p/14101603#M839 
  • Houston AI CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-houston-tx-usa/ec-p/14101612#M840 

Functional programming mini series on Hands-on SAP Dev  

  • Series playlist https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQB-0sK7KSRwCc2gdtlZDIkL  
  • Functional programming resources https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/03/24/functional-programming-resources/  
  • The Macclesfield Canal https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macclesfield_Canal  

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) April release 
02:29 May Developer Challenge Mid-month Promotion 
03:23  Sapphire Promo / Mini CodeJam Roadshow in the US in June 
05:21 Functional programming mini series on Hands-on SAP Dev   

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May 15th, 2025.

[DJ] The April 2025 release of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, that's CAP to us human beings, is out. Before we dive into some of the release notes, let's just take a step back to see the major version release schedule. So the next major release of CAP, that's v9, is due very soon, and it's important for projects to not only be aware of coming changes but also to start to start transitioning and if you haven't done this yet april this april release is a great time to do that as with any new major release there'll be some changes that you'll need to pay attention to especially with things that have been deprecated and only available via backward compatibility flags during the current major release. So looking at the April release notes, the CDS language and compiler topic area is a good example of this. You should switch on the new CDS parser, which has actually been avlable since February this year. Also in the Node.js area, there have been plenty of things that you can and should be enabling now. All these aspects have been released over the course of the current major release that's v8 in the java topic area there are changes and new features to the outbox service there's now support for consumption of a remote odata singleton instances the value of what's specified in the common.text annotation is now searched automatically there's an enhancement relating to the expansion of too many associations from subqueries and there are new arithmetic and string functions supported by CDSQL. Last but not least, in the plugins topic area, the two plugins, Attachment and Change Tracking, now support multi-tenancy. That just about wraps it up for this April 2025 release of CAP, but the key message is, make sure you're ready for V9 that's coming soon.

[Sheena M K] Hello, developers. The SAP Community Escape House for May is already live, and this month's edition is a very interesting topic about extensibility. So this is how it works. You're trapped inside a mystery house, and your task is to solve clues using your ABAP knowledge. Each puzzle that you solve will bring you closer to the escape, and on the way, you'll be learning a lot of interesting concepts on extensibility in the ABAP cloud world. It's a very creative and fun-filled way to learn new concepts, no slice, just code, logic, and a bit of detective work. Want to give it a try? Head over to the description box for the blog post link and get started today. If you're still stuck or you'd like to share your experience, jump onto the comment box of the community blog post and we are right there to help you out. Happy escaping and good luck.

[Thomas] well i'm here in my backyard and it is a clear sign of spring because we've got a little burrow of baby rabbits in our backyard you know another thing that happens every spring and that is Sapphire so Sapphire 2025 is happening next week be on the lookout for the live stream content that you'll be able to join such as the keynotes but if you aren't able to catch the live event, we'll also have an update on the SAP developer news right here in this channel of all the best announcements and news to come out of Sapphire for developers. Now, it's no spoiler that AI is going to be a big topic at Sapphire this year. I think you'd probably have to be living under a rock not to know that AI is a really hot topic right now. And if you feel as a developer, maybe you've been missing out and need to update your AI skills a little bit, we're also going to help you out there. We have a little mini AI roadshow coming to the U.S. later in June. We're going to take the Generative AI Code Jam on the road, offering it in Newark, in the San Francisco Bay Area, in the Palo Alto offices, and in Houston, Texas. This is all thanks to our friends at Mark9 who are going to be sponsoring these events. So if you are near any of those cities and looking for a little introduction to generative AI that's accessible, regardless of which programming language or environment you're used to working in, check out the schedule in the links below to see if you can join one of those events. so we look forward to sharing more sapphire news with you and bringing more AI related content to you in the form of code jams thanks

[DJ] it's such a lovely day up here on the macclesfield canal that I’ve opened one of the duck hatches anyway just got time to remind you all about our current series exploring functional programming it's on the hands-on sap dev show there are five episodes in fact if you're watching this you're most likely to be watching it at about the same time as the fifth and final episode is airing live but don't worry all the replays are available in this little mini-series. It's just a gentle introduction to functional programming, including covering concepts by hands-on learning by doing, concepts such as functions as values, higher order functions, closures, immutability, currying, point-free coding or tacit coding as it's also known, composition and more. Lots to learn, lots to discover as always, so check out the playlist. The link is in the description. Bye for now. I'll see you next time.

entry.title: Re: Storage type search with wildcard: No entry found for...
entry.content:
Hello @saranyaharik 

Your configuration is correct, but SAP’s standard templates include default settings that are easy to miss when replicating manually. The issue likely resides in one of the following:

  • A missing or misaligned customizing indicator in the manually created storage type.

  • The storage type not properly linked in the removal search sequence or access strategy.

  • A stock/bin availability constraint prevents task creation.

I recommend cross-checking your custom storage type against a working SAP template at the field level in SPRO, including both storage type and removal strategies.

Please let me know if you’d like further support 

Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Workflow translation at user decision step
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a scenario in which workflow translation is not working properly on the 'User decision texts'.

1) We tried to translate the workflow step from English to Japanese from SWDD menu--->Extras-->Translation. But it stops by giving the error as 'Translation is only possible if the logon language is same as the original language'. Here the original language is English and the maintenance language is German.

2) So we translated through SE63 for the three options  Approve, Disapprove and Send back to the original user

3) When we log in with Japanese language and check SWDD-->the user decision step, we can see the corresponding Japanese texts for Approve/Disapprove/Send back to the original user.

But still when we test , the workflow log gives the error as "Translation to language "JA" is missing for step"

Do you know any solution for this? Appreciate your help!

 

 

 

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Create Custom fields to item layout in PR & PO in SAP...
entry.content:
Hello @Neelesh2 

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, users cannot directly add custom fields to the line-item table layout (grid view) of Purchase Requisitions (PR) or Purchase Orders (PO) using standard in-app extensibility tools such as the Custom Fields and Logic app. While it is possible to create and include custom fields in the item detail panel, incorporating them into the line-item list view is not currently achievable through key user extensibility.

Recommendations:

  1. Side-by-Side Extensibility: Utilize the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to develop custom applications or extensions that can display or process the desired custom fields in a tailored UI.

  2. SAP Customer Influence Portal: Submit your requirement to the SAP Customer Influence Portal to suggest this enhancement for future releases.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Automating Comment Backup and Retrieval in SAC – Guidance Needed
entry.content:
Hi Community,
I’m looking for guidance on how to automate the backup and retrieval of comments entered in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)—whether through the comment widget or directly within a story (e.g., charts, tables, or input controls).
Specifically, I’d like to know:

  • Can data actions be written to automate the backup of comments to a separate model or table?
  • If comments are backed up, is it possible to retrieve deleted comments through scripting or data actions?
  • Is there a standard method to export or archive comments in SAC?
  • Are comments stored in a backend model that can be accessed or backed up directly?
  • What are the best practices for maintaining a history of comments for audit, compliance, or collaboration purposes?


Our objective is to build a reliable, automated solution for safeguarding comment data, particularly in planning & reporting scenarios where version control and traceability are critical.
Any insights, examples, or recommendations from your experience would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks in advance,

entry.title: Re: PR: copied line with incorrect G/L accoun
entry.content:
Hello @sumitkakkar12 

When you copy a line item in the Manage Purchase Requisitions app and modify the material, the system does not automatically re-evaluate the GL account. This is normal behaviour, since the copied line keeps the account assignment values from the original.

Recommendation:

To trigger GL account redetermination upon material change:

  • Users must manually clear the account assignment category or GL account field before entering the new material. This prompts the system to re-evaluate the account assignment based on the updated material and valuation class.

Currently, there is no automatic re-determination logic available for this scenario via standard configuration or in-app extensibility.

Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Antony,

Probably this could be related to missing scope item activation or missing steps in the customizing of statutory reports.

1. Review if scope item 1J2 is activated, if not, proceed with the activation.

2. Check on the settings of statutory reporting and follow the documentation in Document and Reporting Compliance | SAP Help Portal

3. Customize the relevant reporting entity as this is a pre-requisite for the task to appear.

Regards,

Tomás

entry.title: Re: Visibility of texts in output management
entry.content:
Hello @Katharinam 

In the output preview, the form template logic and the text determination configuration control the visibility of header or item texts (e.g., TX01—Internal Note, TX02—Sales Note for Customer).
To manage visibility:

  • Go to “Maintain Form Templates” in the Output Management app.
  • Download the form template used (e.g., Sales Quotation).
  • Adjust the form layout in Adobe Lifecycle Designer to exclude internal text types like TX01.

Alternatively, you can implement a business rule or BAdI (SD_BILLING_DOCUMENT_PRINT), if your edition supports it, to filter texts dynamically.

Note: By default, all texts maintained in the document can be printed unless explicitly excluded in the form logic.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Why the following action creation of a sales order is...
entry.content:
Hello @Ingrid_at_Applium 

The follow-up action "Create Sales Order" is unavailable as a standard function in service processing scenarios.

 Suggested Approach:

To track the delivery of tools sent to the customer prior to on-site service:

  • Use Advanced Shipping Notification (ASN) or create a Stock Transport Order (STO) with a delivery reference.

  • Alternatively, leverage service order integration with outbound delivery, if the process allows, to manage tool shipment and monitor status via delivery documents.

For enhanced tracking or customer-specific logistics, side-by-side extensions via SAP BTP may also be considered.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the non stock item catagery in servi...
entry.content:
Hello @Ingrid_at_Applium 

Dear Ingrid,

To activate the non-stock item category in a Service Order for a Solution Order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, please ensure the following:

- The material master has the correct Product Type and Item Category Group (e.g., “LEIS” for services).

- The necessary item category is included in the Item Category Determination logic (accessible through SSCUI 102630, if available).

- The relevant scope items (e.g., 5D2) are activated.

If the item category remains hidden, please raise a ticket with SAP 

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: WalkMe Enablement Has Arrived!
entry.content:
Hello! My name is Mark Anderson and I’m the Business Transformation Management Lead for SAP Partners in SAP Industries & Experience team, otherwise known as SIX.

I’m excited to announce solution enablement for WalkMe is now available!

What Is WalkMe?

WalkMe is a digital adoption platform that overlays existing software applications to provide users with step-by-step guidance, automated workflows, and contextual help. It works by layering in-app guidance directly into your software, ensuring users can navigate through processes with ease. By reducing the need for extensive training and minimizing errors, WalkMe empowers organizations to increase the efficiency and productivity of their workforce.

 

WalkMe offers a range of capabilities that make it an ideal fit for SAP solutions, including:

  • Guided walkthroughs: Users receive real-time prompts and instructions as they complete tasks, making complex processes easier to follow.
  • Automation: WalkMe automates repetitive tasks, allowing users to focus on higher-value activities.
  • Tooltips and pop-ups: Contextual help is provided exactly when and where it is needed, reducing reliance on support teams.
  • Analytics: Data on user interactions with software is collected, providing insights into adoption rates, friction points, and areas for improvement.

How WalkMe Can Enhance SAP Solutions

SAP solutions are known for their robust capabilities, but their complexity can present challenges for new users or those unfamiliar with certain processes. WalkMe’s platform, when integrated with SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Ariba, can help businesses overcome these challenges by guiding users through every step of their workflow. Here’s how WalkMe can enhance key SAP products

 

Available Enablement

Webinar recordings our partners can access on-demand.

  • Accelerate Business Transformation with WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM.
    • Discover how WalkMe helps simplify HR workflows and drives faster adoption of SAP SuccessFactors solutions.
  • Exploring the Latest Innovations in the SAP Integrated Toolchain
    • The SAP Integrated Toolchain includes SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, SAP Cloud ALM, and WalkMe.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition hot topic series: Warehouse Management
    • Choosing between SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and WalkMe Premium for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • Partner Sales Power Up Series: WalkMe
    • Covers commercials and partner opportunities with WalkMe.
    • Japanese session here.

 

Additional WalkMe SAP Community Blogs

  • How WalkMe Works: A Visual Guide to its Deployment Architecture
  • Compensation Statement Acknowledgement Using WalkMe (SAP SuccessFactors HCM)
  • WalkMe – More than a Walk Through (SAP Concur)

 

WalkMe Learning Journeys on SAP Learning Hub

  • Starting your Digital Adoption Journey: WalkMe Fundamentals
  • Getting Started with Building WalkMe Solutions
  • Advancing your Skills in Building WalkMe Solutions

 

WalkMe Certification

  • WalkMe Digital Adoption Consultant

 

Subscribe to this blog to notified when it’s updated with additional content and detail. Comment below on your WalkMe enablement journey or ask a question to our experts.

entry.title: Clean Core as Foundation for Your Cloud Journey
entry.content:
At the DSAG Technology Days 2025 in Wiesbaden, I had the privilege of presenting alongside @michael_sokollek and @Tobi_F (Tobias Fischer) on “Clean Core as Foundation for Your Cloud Journey.” The enthusiastic engagement from the DSAG [1] community—representing over 4,000 companies across the DACH region—affirmed the relevance and timeliness of our enterprise architecture approach to cloud transformation. The presentation followed why, what, and how sections.

 

WHY Clean Core is the Foundation for Cloud Success

Our presentation emphasized that a clean core is not merely a technical objective but a strategic imperative for sustainable innovation:

  • Continuous innovation enablement: Clean core systematically removes barriers to new software capabilities, allowing organizations to adopt emerging technologies and features
  • Process agility through standardization: Companies operating with standardized systems consistently demonstrate superior process agility, underpinned by higher data quality and streamlined operations
  • Reduced complexity and costs: The clean core approach significantly reduces the complexity and costs associated with upgrades, maintenance, and overall system management
  • Strategic S/4HANA transformation foundation: As an ongoing strategic activity rather than a one-time effort, clean core provides the essential groundwork for successful S/4HANA transformation

The audience response was particularly strong when we showcased the correlation between clean core principles and measurable business outcomes. As one CIO from a mid-sized manufacturing company commented: “We’ve been approaching modernization backward—focusing on features first rather than foundation.”

 

WHAT Our EA-RISE Framework Delivers

We explained how our enterprise architecture approach within RISE with SAP delivers comprehensive support throughout the transformation journey:

  • Structured implementation guidance: RISE-EA provides clear pathways through established methods and pre-configured SAP solutions, eliminating guesswork and reducing implementation risk
  • Complexity transformation: The framework transforms architectural complexity into clear business insights, enabling effective decision-making at both strategic and operational levels
  • Accelerated cloud adoption: By providing specialized reference architectures, we create accelerated pathways for cloud adoption tailored to industry-specific needs
  • Strategic alignment: RISE-EA ensures continuous alignment between business objectives and IT implementation across the entire journey, preventing drift between strategic intent and execution

The detailed exploration of greenfield versus brownfield strategies resonated strongly with attendees facing these decisions, particularly our differentiation between “keep clean” strategies for new implementations and “make clean” approaches for system conversions.

 

HOW SAP’s Toolchain Supports Implementation

The demonstration section of our presentation showcased the practical implementation of clean core principles through:

  • Integrated planning ecosystem: SAP’s toolchain enables seamless planning from Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM) to Business Process Management (BPM), providing end-to-end visibility
  • Collaborative modeling: Process analysis fosters stakeholder alignment through structured modeling and governance frameworks that bring business and IT perspectives together
  • Implementation standardization: Reference architectures establish consistent solution deployment standards, reducing variability and accelerating implementation
  • Continuous optimization: The framework enables sustainable optimization across all five clean core dimensions (Extensibility, Data, Integrations, Processes, Operations) through structured feedback loops

Our live demonstration of the Clean Core Scoring Framework, including performance KPIs and governance maturity assessments, provided attendees with tangible tools to measure their progress toward clean core objectives.

 

Impact and Response

The session attracted a diverse audience ranging from mid-sized business process owners to CIOs. The engagement level—particularly during our exploration of process deviation management practices—highlighted the DSAG community’s commitment to moving beyond technical approaches to embrace architectural thinking.

Several attendees approached us afterward to discuss how they could apply our five clean core principles within their specific transformation journeys. The most frequent questions concerned practical governance approaches for maintaining clean core principles in organizations with multiple business units and diverse process owners.

For colleagues who missed the session, the presentation materials are available below. I welcome further conversations about how we can apply these principles with your customers to accelerate their cloud journeys while establishing sustainable architectural foundations.

Special thanks to Michael Sokollek and Tobias Fischer for their excellent collaboration, and to the DSAG organization for providing this platform to showcase SAP’s thought leadership in enterprise architecture.

by Dr. Michael R. Blaschke

[1] The DSAG is one of the most influential SAP user associations in the world. Many dedicated members from over 4,000 companies form a strong network: from medium-sized businesses to DAX-listed corporations, from the specialist department to the CxO level, across all industries, in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland (DACH). The user association is organized into 200 working groups and committees.

entry.title: Export Delivery Data to XML File Using ABAP
entry.content:
Objective:
This blog explains how to fetch delivery header and item data from standard SAP tables LIKP and LIPS, structure it into nested internal tables, convert the data to XML format, and download it as an XML file using ABAP.
Use Case:
⦁ Reading delivery header and item data
⦁ Nesting item data under each header record
⦁ Converting the full structure to XML
⦁ Saving the XML file to the user's local system
Step-by-Step Implementation:
STEP 1: Tables Declaration

TABLES:LIKP,LIPS.

Define a structure for LIPS item data:

TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_LIPS,
         POSNR TYPE LIPS-POSNR,
         PSTYV TYPE LIPS-PSTYV,
         MATNR TYPE LIPS-MATNR,
         MATKL TYPE LIPS-MATKL,
         WERKS TYPE LIPS-WERKS,
         LGORT TYPE LIPS-LGORT,
         SPART TYPE LIPS-SPART,
       END OF TY_LIPS.

TYPES: TY_LIPS_TAB TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_LIPS WITH EMPTY KEY.

DATA: TY_LS_LIPS TYPE TY_LIPS.

Define a final structure for LIKP + LIPS and Define variables for XML creation:

TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
         VBELN    TYPE LIKP-VBELN,
         ERNAM    TYPE LIKP-ERNAM,
         ERZET    TYPE LIKP-ERZET,
         ERDAT    TYPE LIKP-ERDAT,
         VSTEL    TYPE LIKP-VSTEL,
         VKORG    TYPE LIKP-VKORG,
         LFART    TYPE LIKP-LFART,
         LFDAT    TYPE LIKP-LFDAT,
         LT_ITEMS TYPE TY_LIPS_TAB,
       END OF TY_FINAL.

DATA: LT_FINAL TYPE TABLE OF TY_FINAL,
      LS_FINAL TYPE TY_FINAL.

DATA: LT_XML_TAB  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING,
      LV_XML      TYPE STRING,
      LV_FILENAME TYPE STRING.

STEP 2: Selection Screen for Delivery Numbers

SELECT-OPTIONS: SO_VBELN FOR LIKP-VBELN.

STEP 3: Fetch Delivery Header Data (LIKP)

START-OF-SELECTION.

  SELECT VBELN,
         ERNAM,
         ERZET,
         ERDAT,
         VSTEL,
         VKORG,
         LFART,
         LFDAT
    FROM LIKP
    INTO TABLE @DATA(LT_LIKP)
    WHERE VBELN IN @SO_VBELN.

STEP 4: Fetch Delivery Item Data (LIPS)

NOTE: If no data is found, stop execution:

IF LT_LIKP IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No data found for the selected deliveries.' TYPE 'I'.
    EXIT.
  ELSE.

    SELECT VBELN,
           POSNR,
           PSTYV,
           MATNR,
           MATKL,
           WERKS,
           LGORT,
           SPART
      FROM LIPS
      INTO TABLE @DATA(LT_LIPS)
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_LIKP
      WHERE VBELN = @LT_LIKP-VBELN.
  ENDIF.

STEP 5: Merge Header and Item Data into Final Structure

LOOP AT LT_LIKP INTO DATA(LS_LIKP).
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING LS_LIKP TO LS_FINAL." moving the header data into final structure

    LOOP AT LT_LIPS INTO DATA(LS_LIPS) WHERE VBELN = LS_LIKP-VBELN.
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING LS_LIPS TO TY_LS_LIPS." moving the item to temporary item structure
      APPEND TY_LS_LIPS TO LS_FINAL-LT_ITEMS."appending the items data to final item table
    ENDLOOP.

    APPEND LS_FINAL TO LT_FINAL."appending the header and item data
"into final table for download
    CLEAR LS_FINAL.
  ENDLOOP.

STEP 6: Convert Final Internal Table to XML

NOTE: The built-in transformation ID converts the internal table to standard XML.
The root node becomes the parent of all deliveries.

  " Convert to XML
  CALL TRANSFORMATION ID
    SOURCE ROOT = LT_FINAL
    RESULT XML LV_XML.

STEP 7: Split XML into Lines for File Output.

  " Split XML string into lines
  SPLIT LV_XML AT CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>NEWLINE INTO TABLE LT_XML_TAB.

STEP 8: Download the XML to Local PC.

" Output file name
  LV_FILENAME = 'C:\\Users\\nasirp\\Documents\\DEL DET XML FILE\\delivery_data.xml'.

  " Download to XML file
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      FILENAME                = LV_FILENAME
      FILETYPE                = 'ASC'
    TABLES
      DATA_TAB                = LT_XML_TAB
    EXCEPTIONS
      FILE_WRITE_ERROR        = 1
      NO_BATCH                = 2
      GUI_REFUSE_FILETRANSFER = 3
      INVALID_TYPE            = 4
      NO_AUTHORITY            = 5
      UNKNOWN_ERROR           = 6
      OTHERS                  = 7.

STEP 9: Handle Success/Error Messages.

  IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
    MESSAGE 'XML file downloaded successfully.' TYPE 'S'.
  ELSE.
    MESSAGE 'Error during file download.' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

Output Example:

* Input Screen: 

After Execute it will Download in the format of XML in local file (Which has given path)

entry.title: Exporting Multilingual Translations from ABAP to UTF-8 CSV Format
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we’ll learn how to export multilingual translation data into a CSV file with UTF-8 encoding using ABAP. This ensures that special characters from various languages are preserved and correctly displayed in Excel or Notepad.

🔹1. Define the Structure for Translations

Here, we define a structure TY_TRANSLATION with fields for each language. Each field is of type STRING to support multilingual characters (e.g., accents, umlauts).

🔹2. Declare Data Objects

  • LT_TRANSLATION – Table to store all translation entries.
  • LS_TRANSLATION – Work area for one row.
  • LT_OUTPUT – Table to store final CSV content.
  • LV_LINE – Line to hold concatenated fields for CSV
  • LV_SEPARATOR – Delimiter (comma for CSV).

🔹3. Fill Sample Translation Data

This section assigns translated words to each language and adds the entry to the internal table.

🔹4. Add UTF-8 BOM (Byte Order Mark)

Why? Without the BOM, Excel might not recognize the file as UTF-8 and misinterpret special characters. The BOM ensures the file opens correctly in tools like Excel.

🔹5. Add Header Row to CSV

Adds a readable header row for the CSV file.

🔹6. Create CSV Content from Internal Table

Each translation line is merged into one string using commas and added to the output table.

🔹7. Download the CSV File

This function downloads the CSV to the desktop. The CODEPAGE = 4110 ensures UTF-8 encoding.

🔹8. Success/Failure Message

After attempting the download, the program shows a success or failure message.

REPORT ZLANG_DOWNLOAD_FILE2.

TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_TRANSLATION,
         SPANISH     TYPE STRING,
         PORTUGUESE TYPE STRING,
         GERMAN      TYPE STRING,
         FRENCH      TYPE STRING,
         ITALIAN     TYPE STRING,
         DUTCH       TYPE STRING,
         GREEK       TYPE STRING,
         TURKISH     TYPE STRING,
         CZECH       TYPE STRING,
         POLISH      TYPE STRING,
         NORWEGIAN   TYPE STRING,
         SWEDISH     TYPE STRING,
         SLOVAK      TYPE STRING,
         HUNGARIAN   TYPE STRING,
         DANISH      TYPE STRING,
       END OF TY_TRANSLATION.

DATA: LT_TRANSLATION TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_TRANSLATION,
      LS_TRANSLATION TYPE TY_TRANSLATION,
      LT_OUTPUT      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF STRING,
      LV_LINE        TYPE STRING,
      LV_SEPARATOR   TYPE C VALUE ','.

* Fill one translation line
LS_TRANSLATION-SPANISH    = 'Tapa extremo'.
LS_TRANSLATION-PORTUGUESE = 'Tampa terminal'.
LS_TRANSLATION-GERMAN     = 'Endkappe'.
LS_TRANSLATION-FRENCH     = 'Capuchon extrémité tappo terminale'.
LS_TRANSLATION-ITALIAN    = 'Einddop'.
LS_TRANSLATION-DUTCH      = 'Ακροκάλυμμα'.
LS_TRANSLATION-GREEK      = 'Uç kapağı'.
LS_TRANSLATION-TURKISH    = 'Koncová krytka'.
LS_TRANSLATION-CZECH      = 'Zatyczka'.
LS_TRANSLATION-POLISH     = 'Zatyczka'.
LS_TRANSLATION-NORWEGIAN  = 'Ändkåpa'.
LS_TRANSLATION-SWEDISH    = 'Koncový kryt'.
LS_TRANSLATION-SLOVAK     = 'Végzáró sapka'.
LS_TRANSLATION-HUNGARIAN  = 'Endehætte'.
LS_TRANSLATION-DANISH     = 'poykorkki'.

APPEND LS_TRANSLATION TO LT_TRANSLATION.

* Add UTF-8 BOM (to make Excel recognize encoding properly)
DATA: LV_BOM TYPE STRING.
LV_BOM = cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>uccp( 'FEFF' ).
APPEND LV_BOM TO LT_OUTPUT.

* Optional: Add CSV header
APPEND 'Spanish,Portuguese,German,French,Italian,Dutch,Greek,Turkish,Czech,Polish,Norwegian,Swedish,Slovak,Hungarian,Danish'
       TO LT_OUTPUT.

* Fill content
LOOP AT LT_TRANSLATION INTO LS_TRANSLATION.
  CONCATENATE LS_TRANSLATION-SPANISH
              LS_TRANSLATION-PORTUGUESE
              LS_TRANSLATION-GERMAN
              LS_TRANSLATION-FRENCH
              LS_TRANSLATION-ITALIAN
              LS_TRANSLATION-DUTCH
              LS_TRANSLATION-GREEK
              LS_TRANSLATION-TURKISH
              LS_TRANSLATION-CZECH
              LS_TRANSLATION-POLISH
              LS_TRANSLATION-NORWEGIAN
              LS_TRANSLATION-SWEDISH
              LS_TRANSLATION-SLOVAK
              LS_TRANSLATION-HUNGARIAN
              LS_TRANSLATION-DANISH
              INTO LV_LINE SEPARATED BY LV_SEPARATOR.
  APPEND LV_LINE TO LT_OUTPUT.
ENDLOOP.

* Download the CSV file in UTF-8 with BOM
CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
  EXPORTING
    FILENAME              = 'C:\Users\naveenda\Desktop\Welcome_f.csv'
    FILETYPE              = 'ASC'
    CODEPAGE              = '4110'  " UTF-8
  TABLES
    DATA_TAB              = LT_OUTPUT
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS                = 1.

IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
  WRITE: / '✅ File downloaded successfully to desktop.'.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / '❌ Download failed.'.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Custom field with multiline in S4 public cloud-key user extensibility
entry.content:
I might be missing something, but are you sure this is the right approach?

① You mentioned "S4 public cloud using key user extensibility". In the very next statement, you mentioned "enabled the field in SAP Gui screen"⚠️S/4 Public Cloud isn't accessible via SAP GUI screen.

② If in-fact you are using SAP Gui screen via WebGUI, then, Gui doesn't inherently support Multi-line fields. It uses Text controls (and associated extra logic) to display Multi-line fields. So, you can't add a Multi-line field to a Gui screen, just be dragging and dropping.

③ Most business contexts have size limits on how much you can extend. If you use up 500 characters in just one field, that can hurt you in the future. So, re-check the real need for such a long field extension and try to find another solution for your problem (and, there are always other solutions)

The field you have added is at Business Partner level and not at BP + Tax Category Level.

Hence, the field you have added can only hold ONE value per Customer and not individual values for each row in Tax data tab.

entry.content:
Hi Phillip,

I would recommend, building the UI using the Build Apps. A FIORI like application that can be accessed through the launchpad.

This gives you better flexibility and UI than the Form builder in the BPA tenant.

On submit of the form in the app, you can call the BPA API trigger, which initiates the entire process for you.

Let me know if this helps.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Re: How to Trigger a Process in SAP Build Process Automat...
entry.content:
You would need to identify the event based on which the entire process should be started.

Broadly, you should be able to categorize this into 3 categories:

1. Is it an event which is initiated by a specific person (You might want to create an attended trigger to start the automation process or, based on the design, ask the user to enter details in a form, which on submission triggers the process)

2. Is the process supposed to be started on a fixed time (Identify the schedule, create a scheduled trigger based on the schedule to trigger your automations)

3. If it is not one of the above, then this means, that the process has to be started based on some other action being performed. You would need to essentially create an API trigger, which is called by the event in whichever system started the entire process.

Do let me know if this helps you.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Automating Input Tax Credit Distribution through Input Service Distributor (ISD) Mechanism using SAP
entry.content:
In India’s GST system, many companies use a shared services model. This means that departments like HR, IT, legal, or finance offer services to multiple business locations (branches). But often, invoices for these services are billed only to one location, such as the head office. The challenge is: how do you share the input tax credit (ITC) from these invoices with the branches that actually used the service?

This is where Input Service Distributor (ISD) becomes important.

In this blog, I will first explain what ISD means under GST law and then walk you through how to implement it in SAP systems, both technically and functionally. This will help finance and IT teams to make sure they stay compliant, while automating the process effectively.

What is ISD Under GST?

ISD is a special type of GST registration that allows one office (usually the head office) to receive GST invoices for services used across multiple branches and then distribute the input tax credit (ITC) to those branches in proportion to their turnover.

This is defined under Section 2(61) of the CGST Act and guided by Rule 39 of the CGST Rules.

Please note: 

  1. ISD applies only to input services, not goods or capital goods.
  2. The Govt of India has made ISD provisions mandatory from April 1, 2025, via notification No. 16/2024 – Central Tax, dated the 6thAugust 2024.

Key Features and Conditions:

  1. ISD must have a separate GST registration, different from normal branch GSTINs.
  2. Only common input services are eligible for distribution.
  3. ITC must be shared based on the turnover of each branch.
  4. Distribution must be done via proper documentation and reported in GSTR-6 every month.
  5. All branches must be under the same PAN (Permanent Account Number).

Example: How ITC Distribution Works

Example 1: Company HQ has incurred an audit expense of INR 100000 and paid GST of INR 18000 e.g. 18% (9%CGST + 9%SGST) on the expense. The audit was for Pune and Delhi location. Below will be the ITC distribution:

ISD Branch

Receipt Branch

Branch Turnover

Total Turnover

Ratio (Branch Turnover/Total Turnover)

ITC Amount (Total Credit*Ratio)

Receipt Tax Type

Mumbai

Pune

6000000

10000000

60%

10,800

CGST+SGST

Mumbai

Delhi

4000000

10000000

40%

7,200

IGST

How ISD is Handled in SAP?

If you need step-by-step instructions with screenshots, refer to the detailed papers I’ve written on ISD configuration in SAP. These are free to download. These guides explain:

  • How ITC is handled using ISD
  • Full setup for both SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA

ISD Conceptual Overview and SAP Technical Implementation Steps

Provides a clear conceptual understanding of the ISD mechanism along with detailed, screenshot-based instructions for technical configuration in SAP.

ISD Conceptual Overview and SAP Technical Implementation Steps

ISD SAP Functional Configuration and Operational Execution

Offers step-by-step guidance on functional ISD setup in SAP and explains how to execute ISD transactions effectively within the system.

ISD SAP Functional Configuration and Operational Execution

I am also giving reference steps in this blog below on How to Handle ISD configurations in SAP.

SAP offers a standard solution for handling ISD under its India Localization package. The setup includes two phases:

  1. Technical Implementation
  2. Functional Implementation

Below are the steps for technical and functional implementation.

  1. Technical Implementation:

This is the first step in implementing the ISD solution in SAP. It involves setting up the required prerequisites and applying the necessary SAP OSS notes to enable the required functionality in SAP. 

  1. Prerequisites:

It is important to make sure the system meets all the prerequisite requirements.

  • Minimum support pack:

To implement Input Service Distribution, the SAP system must be at least on the below support pack mentioned in SAP note 1175384

AP_APPL Release

Support Pack

SAP ERP 6.0 (600)

SP 26

EHP2 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 (602)

SP 16

EHP3 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 (603)

SP 15

EHP4 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 (604)

SP 16

EHP5 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 (605)

SP 13

EHP6 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 (606)

SP 14

EHP6 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 for HANA (616- SAP HANA)

SP 08

EHP7 FOR SAP ERP 6.0(617)

SP 07

EHP8 FOR SAP ERP 6.0(618)

SP 02

SAP S/4HANA ON-PREMISE 1511

SP 02

  • Create Functional Group:

It is mandatory to create the ISD function group, J_1IG_ISDN, before starting the ISD implementation using transaction code SE80.

  1. Implement Necessary SAP Notes to Activate Functionality:

The next step is to ensure that the required SAP notes are implemented in the SAP system.

  • Data Dictionary:

SAP introduced a new standard solution for ISD in 2018 and updated some of the existing SAP tables with additional data dictionary objects. To check the applicability of these additional data dictionary objects, please refer to SAP note 2620349 for ECC clients and SAP note 2676848 for SAP S4 Hana clients.

  • Implement OSS Notes for new ISD functionality:

Perform the manual steps as outlined in OSS note 2621654 (for ECC Clients) or 2677060 (for S4 Hana) to enable the new ISD functionality in SAP.

  1. Activation of Service

Below are the configuration steps in SAP to activate the latest India Localization solution.

  • Activate Country Version for India:

Follow the IMG path below to activate the India localization component in SAP:

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Activate Local Version India for Specific Fiscal Years.

  • Activate Business Transaction Event:

Follow the IMG path below to activate the Business Transaction Event (BTE) for India Localization:

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG à Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings à India --> Activate Business Transaction Events.

II. Functional Implementation:

This is the next step in implementing the ISD solution in SAP. It includes activation, functional configuration, and ODN configuration to enable the ISD functionality in SAP

  1. Functional Configuration

Below are the functional configuration steps to enable ISD for India.  

  • Creation of ISD business place

In SAP, all branches of a company are set up as business places

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India à General Settings --> Define Business Places.

  • Creation of an ISD plant

Each business place is linked to a plant, though it is not necessary to create a separate plant for each business place.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Logistics – General --> Define, copy, delete, check plant.

  • Assigning ISD Business place to ISD Plant

Assign the newly created plant to the ISD business place by following the below IMG path.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> General Settings --> Assign Business Places to Plants.

  • Creation of ISD Document Type

It is recommended to create a separate document type in SAP to distribute Input Tax Credit (ITC) from the ISD branch to other branches of the organization.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Document --> Basic Settings --> Document Types --> Define Document Types for Entry View.

  • Assign ISD Document type to ISD transfer postings.

Assign the ISD document type to the ISD process using the IMG path below.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Specify Document Types.

  • Map ISD Business Places to Receiving Business Places (J_1IG_ISD_MAP_V)

This is an important configuration to assign the ISD branch to the receiving branch.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India à Goods and Services Tax --> Input Service Distributor --> Map ISD Business Places to Receiving Business Places.

  • Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places (J_1IG_ISD_TO_V)

This is a recurring activity where the turnover of each branch needs to be updated annually.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings à India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Input Service Distributor --> Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places.

  • Create ISD GL accounts for CGST, SGST, and IGST.

It is recommended to create a separate set of Tax GL accounts for ISD.

IMG Path: SAP East Access à SAP Menu --> Accounting --> Financial Accounting --> General Ledger --> Master Records --> G/L Accounts à Individual Processing --> FS00 - Centrally

  • Create a new ISD tax code activating all three GST tax conditions e.g. three conditions - JIS, JIC, JII

A new tax code needs to be created for ISD to distribute the ITC. This tax code will have all three GST tax conditions—CGST, SGST, and IGST—activated.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Calculation --> Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases.

  • Configure ISD Invoice Posting Settings (J_1IG_ISD_POST_V)

In the next customization, please maintain the ISD GL accounts created in step h for ISD tax posting

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Input Service Distributor --> Configure ISD Invoice Posting Settings.

  • Maintain default tax code

In the next customization, please maintain the ISD tax code created in step I above in the default tax code settings.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India à Goods and Services Tax --> Input Service Distributor à Configure ISD Invoice Posting Settings.

  1. Official document number (ODN) Configuration:
    • Maintain Document Classes

The first step is to create a 3-digit document class to set up ODN for the ISD branch.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering --> Maintain Document Classes.

  • Assign Document Types to Document Classes

The next step is to maintain default document type for the document class using the below IMG path.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering --> Assign Document Types to Document Classes.

  • Maintain Number Groups

The following setting is used to create a 6-digit ISD number group.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering à Maintain Number Groups.

  • Maintain Number Ranges

The next step is to maintain the ODN number range for the ISD branch. Please make sure to select the object as J_1IG_ODN and the number group as GISD01.

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India -->Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering --> Maintain Number Ranges.

  • Assign Number Ranges to Business Places (Table J_1IG_OFNUM)

The final step in setting up ODN for ISD is to activate and assign the ODN number range to the ISD business place using the IMG path below

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG --> Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering --> Assign Number Ranges to Business Places.

  • The fiscal year check for the distribution for the ISD formula (April to March) for India will be considered by the below configuration

India follows a fiscal year from April to March. The turnover and ODN number range customization are maintained by the calendar year. The customization below links the calendar fiscal year with India’s fiscal year

IMG Path: SPRO --> IMG à Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting Global Settings --> Tax on Sales/Purchases --> Basic Settings --> India --> Goods and Services Tax --> Cross-Application Document Numbering à Maintain Fiscal Year Variant for ODN.

Read in Detail at Below Paper:

Once again, if you’d like to explore the ISD mechanism further and need step-by-step, screenshot-based instructions to implement it in SAP, refer to the two freely available papers below:

ISD Conceptual Overview and SAP Technical Implementation Steps

Explains the ISD concept under GST and provides detailed technical configuration steps for SAP readiness.

ISD Conceptual Overview and SAP Technical Implementation Steps

ISD SAP Functional Configuration and Operational Execution

Guides you through the complete functional setup and execution of ISD transactions in SAP.

ISD SAP Functional Configuration and Operational Execution

Conclusion:

With ISD becoming mandatory from April 1, 2025, businesses must ensure seamless ITC distribution across branches using standard SAP features. SAP’s India Localization offers a comprehensive solution to automate and comply with ISD requirements. Proper technical and functional setup in SAP not only ensures GST compliance but also simplifies ITC allocation, making the process efficient and error-free.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Webinar のご案内 ( Welcome セッション／ Configueration Webinar シリーズ )
entry.content:
現在SAPでは、SAP Cloud ALM に関する Webinar を定期的に開催しており、日本のお客様、パートナー様向けにはグローバルで開催される各セッションを日本語で提供いたします。

このブログ記事では、その各セッションの概要、およびその開催スケジュールについてご案内しています。

Integrated Toolchain の中核となる SAP Cloud ALM に関する情報をタイムリーに収集できる絶好の機会となります。多くのお客様、パートナー様のご参加をお待ちしております。

SAP Cloud ALM Welcome セッション

このセッションでは、SAP Cloud ALM のコンセプトや機能概要と合わせ、SAP Cloud ALM のプロビジョニング後の初回アクセス方法や SAP Cloud ALM の初期設定方法についても説明いたします。
SAP Cloud ALM の概要をまず押さえたい方、SAP Cloud ALM のプロビジョニングを完了しその後の利用準備に取り掛かっている方など、SAP Cloud ALM の活用に向けてスタートしたいお客様、パートナー様にとって最適なセッションです。

対象者：SAP Cloud ALM に関する基本的な情報を収集、理解したい方、SAP Cloud ALMのプロビジョニングを完了、もしくは直近で予定されている方

セッションの目的： SAP Cloud ALMの機能概要の理解、およびプロビジョニング後に必要となる各種情報、設定方法の理解

アジェンダ：

  • SAP Cloud ALMへの初回アクセス方法
  • ユーザの追加と初期設定の方法
  • 機能概要のご紹介とデモ
  • 役立つ資料やサイトのご案内
  • Q&A

今後の開催スケジュールは以下となります。

各セッションへの申し込みは、以下テーブル内の「Register Now」のリンクからご登録可能です。

（スケジュールが確定次第、お申し込み先への情報含め順次更新予定です。）

開催日時お申し込み先
5月23日（金）10:00 - 11:30（日本時間）Register Now
6月20日（金）10:00 - 11:30（日本時間）Register Now

過去に提供したコンテンツはこちらです。

レコーディング／セッション資料

 

SAP Cloud ALM Configuration Webinar シリーズ

このセッションは、SAP Cloud ALM の機能を実際に活用するための各種情報を提供することを目的としています。主に、エンドツーエンドでの活用方法、各機能の設定方法などをトピック毎に提供いたします。
SAP Cloud ALM を本格的に活用していくことをご検討されている方にとって最適なセッションです。

対象者：導入プロジェクトに関わる方々（プロジェクトマネージャ／リーダー、 プロジェクトメンバー、など）、システム運用に関わる方々（運用責任者、運用チームリーダー、運用メンバー、など）

セッションの目的： SAP Cloud ALM の機能有効化、設定方法、および利用方法の理解

各セッションの開催スケジュールは以下となります。

各セッションへの申し込みは、以下テーブル内の「Register Now」のリンクからご登録可能です。

entry.title: Significance of Origin Key in BTP Trust, especially sap.custom
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are about to onboard SAP Digital Manufacturing. With this comes BTP and establishing trust between subaccount and Cloud Integration Services.

That being said, when using the "Establish Trust" wizard, the default value for "Origin Key" will be "sap.custom".

I know about the overall posibility to use multiple Origin Key values to separate different IdPs / trusts within one subaccount, something that is feasible but not the norm, see Using Multiple Identity Providers from the Same Subaccount, however, I'm unsure about the significance of the wizard's default value "sap.custom"

My questions

  1. Is there some kind of built in "fallback" or hard wired lookup for "sap.custom"? In other words, would it be a bad idea to not have at least one entry with "sap.custom" (providing we are going the route of using Cloud Identity Services and not the default IdP)
  2. For our use case we have SAP Digital Manufacturing deployed in the subaccount and nothing more. Am I right that in this case it might be just fine to stay with the "sap.custom" moniker or is it (when planning ahead) a good idea to apply a more meaningful naming convention? And if so, what should I name the darn thing then? cis_dev (for cloud identity services development environment / tier) or something else?

Many thanks and cheers

J
